title: Fall protection - Duty to have fall protection
text: Each employee on a walking/working surface (horizontal and vertical surface) with an unprotected side or edge which is 6 feet (1.8 m) or more above a lower level shall be protected from falling by the use of guardrail systems, safety net systems, or personal fall arrest systems.

title: Scaffold general requirements
text: Capacity 4× max intended load. Guardrails on all open sides + ends of platforms above 10 ft. Inspected by competent person before each work shift + after any modification. Tagged: green tag = ready, yellow = caution + restriction, red = not ready / do not use.

title: Excavation - Requirements for protective systems
text: Each employee in an excavation shall be protected from cave-ins by an adequate protective system designed (sloping, benching, support system, shield system) UNLESS the excavation is < 5 ft AND examination of the ground by a competent person indicates no cave-in potential, OR the excavation is made entirely in stable rock.

title: Personal protective equipment - Criteria
text: Protective equipment, including PPE for eyes, face, head, extremities, etc., shall be provided, used, and maintained in a sanitary and reliable condition wherever it is necessary by reason of hazards. Hazard assessment + training required.

title: Wiring design and protection
text: Lockout/tagout required for service or maintenance of electrical equipment. Energized work prohibited unless de-energizing introduces additional / increased hazards OR infeasible due to equipment design / operational limitations — and only by qualified persons with an Energized Electrical Work Permit.

title: Cranes and Derricks in Construction
text: Subpart CC. Requirements for crane operator qualification + certification, signal-person qualification, rigger qualification, ground conditions, assembly/disassembly, power-line safety, inspection.

title: Respirable crystalline silica
text: Silica exposure controls for construction. Table 1 lists specified exposure-control methods per task (e.g. cutting / grinding / drilling). Compliance via Table 1 OR alternative exposure assessment with engineering / work-practice controls. Action level 25 μg/m³; PEL 50 μg/m³ 8-hr TWA.

title: Confined Spaces in Construction
text: Subpart AA. Permit-required confined space program with: pre-entry test (oxygen 19.5-23.5%, LEL < 10%, toxics < PEL), continuous monitoring, attendant (entry watch), rescue plan, communication, retrieval equipment.

Falls = #1 cause of construction fatalities (~35% of construction deaths annually). Prevention: identify height ≥ 6 ft, fall-protection plan, personal fall arrest system (full-body harness + shock-absorbing lanyard + anchor 5000 lb), guardrails, safety nets, training, competent person.

Struck-by = #2 cause. Common sources: vehicles, falling objects, flying particles, swinging loads. Prevention: hi-vis PPE, hard hats, eye protection, exclusion zones around equipment, controlled lifting paths, tag lines on loads.

Caught-in/between = #3 cause. Common sources: trench collapse, machinery, crushed between equipment + walls. Prevention: trenching protective systems, machine guards, awareness training, exclusion zones.

Electrocution = #4 cause. Common sources: contact with overhead power lines, contact with energized circuits, no lockout/tagout. Prevention: 10-foot rule for power lines, GFCI on construction sites, qualified electrical workers, LOTO.

Competent person = one capable of identifying existing and predictable hazards in the surroundings, or working conditions which are unsanitary, hazardous, or dangerous to employees, AND who has authorization to take prompt corrective measures. Required by name for: excavation, scaffolding, fall protection, rigging, demolition, confined spaces, asbestos, lead, silica.

title: Occupational Health and Safety Management Systems
text: ANSI/ASSP Z10.0 framework based on Plan-Do-Check-Act. Components: leadership commitment, planning (hazard identification + risk assessment), implementation + operation (controls, training, communication), evaluation + corrective action, management review.
